sentence: Subjective:
The patient, Stephanie Morales, is a 44-year-old female who presents today 6 weeks status post right medial unicompartmental arthroplasty.
She reports that the knee is treating her well, with no problems, but mentions numbness on the side of the knee.
She states that the numbness is still present but acknowledges that it will improve as the nerves heal.
Past medical history:
The patient underwent right medial unicompartmental arthroplasty 6 weeks ago.
Review of systems:
The patient reports numbness on the side of the knee, which is expected to improve as the nerves heal.
Current medications:
[NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam:
The patient's gait was antalgic with a slight limp and decreased velocity, but she is not using an assistive device.
The right knee focused exam reveals a healed surgical scar, mild swelling, and tenderness along the area of the incision.
Range of motion is 0 to 120 degrees, with everything else normal.
Test Results:
Radiographs of the right knee, taken today, show implants in a stable position with no evidence of loosening or early complications.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient is a 6-week postoperative status following right medial unicompartmental arthroplasty with expected improvement in numbness as the nerves heal.
Problem list: Right knee postoperative; numbness on the side of the knee; unknown.
Plan:
I instructed the patient to continue with physical therapy to work on strength, range of motion, and endurance.
I also told her to expect aches and pains occasionally, which should resolve in 2-3 months.
I prescribed a note to excuse her from work for another 6 weeks, with specific restrictions on lifting, standing, and sitting.
I also informed her that driving will be determined by her own readiness and feeling safe to do so.
I will see her again in 6 weeks for a telemedicine visit to check on her progress.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen again in 6 weeks for a telemedicine visit to check on her progress and discuss her job status further.
